title: hypericum mutilum
intro: Hypericum mutilum, also known as St. Andrew's Cross, is a low-growing, annual or perennial plant native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Hypericum mutilum include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve wound healing, and provide relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be dried and used in infusions or added to topical creams and ointments.
